Metric: Software name? (string)
URL? (URL)
Affiliation (institution(s)) (string or N/A)
Response: Carnegie Mellon University Robotics Institute / Quality of Life Technology Center (originated 2006); later maintained at JSK Robotics Lab, University of Tokyo; primary maintainer: Rosen Diankov (MUJIN Inc.)

Metric: Software purpose (string)
Response: Open-source robotics planning framework for testing, developing, and deploying motion planning algorithms; focuses on simulation and analysis of kinematic and geometric information for robot motion planning, especially manipulation

Response: C++, Python, other (Octave/MATLAB bindings; plugin interface supports other languages)

Metric: Is there evidence that performance was considered? Performance refers to either speed, storage, or throughput. (yes∗, no)
Additional comments? (can cover any metrics you feel are missing, or any other thoughts you have)
Response: OpenRAVE began as a rewrite of the RAVE simulator (developed by James Kuffner, 1995) by Rosen Diankov at CMU in 2006. It uses a plugin architecture for planners, controllers, and sensors. Stable release is 0.8.2 (2012); current GitHub code is more recent but formal versioning lags. Widely used in manipulation and grasping research. A Python 3 compatible fork (openrave3) exists on GitHub.

Section: Installability (Measured via installation on a virtual machine.)
Metric: Additional comments? (can cover any metrics you feel are missing, or any other thoughts you have)
Response: Installation is complex and time-consuming. Requires building from source with many system dependencies. The documentation is fragmented across the official website and community wikis. Compatibility with newer Ubuntu versions (22.04+) requires additional patches. Docker-based installation is a simpler alternative but requires Docker knowledge. This is one of the more difficult installations among the simulators reviewed.

Response: The grasping and manipulation tutorial examples ran correctly after installation. No CI system is active in the repository. The doctoral thesis provides a strong theoretical grounding for the implemented algorithms.

Response: A minor compilation error was encountered due to API changes in system libraries, requiring a community patch. Once installed, the runtime was stable. This reflects the challenge of maintaining compatibility with an aging codebase against evolving system libraries.

Response: Error messages are generally informative, though some low-level C++ errors surface through Python with less context. COLLADA model loading errors include file path and parse location information.

Response: GitHub Issues (rdiankov/openrave), openrave-users mailing list, openrave.org documentation wiki, Stack Overflow (openrave tag)

Metric: Overall impression? (1 .. 10)
Additional comments? (can cover any metrics you feel are missing, or any other thoughts you have)
Response: Documentation was comprehensive at peak activity but has not kept pace with code changes. Community activity has decreased significantly. The mailing list and GitHub Issues are less active than in the software's peak years. Primarily targets experienced robotics researchers.

Response: Reduced maintenance activity in recent years. 10,375 total commits reflect a long development history but activity has declined. No formal contribution guide. Many open issues remain unaddressed. The Python 3 fork (openrave3) represents community-driven maintenance.

Metric: Is API documented? (yes, no, n/a)
Overall impression? (1 .. 10)
Additional comments? (can cover any metrics you feel are missing, or any other thoughts you have)
Response: Highly modular plugin architecture: planners, controllers, sensors, and IK solvers are all plugins. IKFast is a notable sub-tool for generating fast analytical IK solutions. Python API allows scripting and integration with other systems. Supports COLLADA and URDF robot models.

Response: Code is generally readable and well-organized into modules (planning, kinematics, collision, rendering). Some older C++ files have inconsistent comment density. The plugin architecture aids modularity. A few numerical constants are hard-coded without named definitions.
